perturbation.original_text: 2.5. PAYMENT. 2.5.1. FEES. The fee for the placements and other rights provided under this Agreement for the Initial Term (as defined below) is * * * is payable within * * * of the date of this Agreement, with the balance of such fee payable by Sponsor in * * * consecutive equal installments of * * * payable by the * * * of the Initial Term commencing * * * following * * * the Launch Date (as defined below).
perturbation.changed_text: 2.5. PAYMENT. 2.5.1. FEES. The fee for the placements and other rights provided under this Agreement for the Initial Term (as defined below) is * * * is payable within 10 days of the date of this Agreement, with the balance of such fee payable by Sponsor in * * * consecutive equal installments of * * * payable by the * * * of the Initial Term commencing * * * following * * * the Launch Date (as defined below). However, due to the nature of the sponsorship, Vitamin Shoppe has the right to defer all payments until 30 days after the end of the initial term.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the fee is payable within 10 days of the agreement date, but then grants Vitamin Shoppe the right to defer ALL payments until 30 days after the end of the initial term. This creates a clear contradiction regarding the payment deadline.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.5. PAYMENT. 2.5.1. FEES. The fee for the placements and other rights provided under this Agreement for the Initial Term (as defined below) is * * * is payable within 10 days of the date of this Agreement, with the balance of such fee payable by Sponsor in * * * consecutive equal installments of * * * payable by the * * * of the Initial Term commencing * * * following * * * the Launch Date (as defined below). However, due to the nature of the sponsorship, Vitamin Shoppe has the right to defer all payments until 30 days after the end of the initial term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating the fee is payable within 10 days, but then allows Vitamin Shoppe to defer all payments until 30 days after the initial term ends. This directly conflicts with the initial payment deadline and creates uncertainty regarding the payment terms, thus falling under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition because different sections (in this case, parts of the same section) provide conflicting deadlines. The contradictory text is in the same location as the contradictory text, which still makes it a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9.3. ASSIGNMENT. Neither party may assign this Agreement, in whole or in part, without the other party's written consent, which consent will not be unreasonably withheld, except that: (a) a party's rights and obligation hereunder may be transferred to a successor of all or substantially all of the business and assets of the party regardless of how the transaction or series of related transactions is structured, provided, that the successor party agrees to be bound by all of the terms and conditions of this Agreement; and (b) Sponsor may assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement to any entity (i) which operates the Sponsor Website and (ii) which agrees to bound by all of the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.3. ASSIGNMENT. Neither party may assign this Agreement, in whole or in part, without the other party's written consent, which consent will not be unreasonably withheld, except that: (a) a party's rights and obligation hereunder may be transferred to a successor of all or substantially all of the business and assets of the party regardless of how the transaction or series of related transactions is structured, provided, that the successor party agrees to be bound by all of the terms and conditions of this Agreement; and (b) Sponsor may assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement to any entity (i) which operates the Sponsor Website and (ii) which agrees to bound by all of the terms and conditions of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Drkoop.com reserves the right to freely assign this agreement to any third party without consent.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction. The first sentence of section 9.3 states that neither party can assign the agreement without written consent. The added sentence states Drkoop.com can freely assign it without consent. This directly contradicts the initial clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.3. ASSIGNMENT. Neither party may assign this Agreement, in whole or in part, without the other party's written consent, which consent will not be unreasonably withheld, except that: (a) a party's rights and obligation hereunder may be transferred to a successor of all or substantially all of the business and assets of the party regardless of how the transaction or series of related transactions is structured, provided, that the successor party agrees to be bound by all of the terms and conditions of this Agreement; and (b) Sponsor may assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement to any entity (i) which operates the Sponsor Website and (ii) which agrees to bound by all of the terms and conditions of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Drkoop.com reserves the right to freely assign this agreement to any third party without consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text of section 9.3 states that 'Neither party may assign this Agreement, in whole or in part, without the other party's written consent'. The changed text adds a sentence stating 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, Drkoop.com reserves the right to freely assign this agreement to any third party without consent.' This directly contradicts the initial clause of section 9.3 as it allows Drkoop.com to assign the agreement without consent, while the initial clause requires consent from both parties for any assignment. The contradicted text is located within the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: 8.4. TERMINATION FOR CAUSE. Either party may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' written notice of a breach by the other party, provided such breach is not cured within such thirty-day period.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.4. TERMINATION FOR CAUSE. Either party may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' written notice of a breach by the other party, provided such breach is not cured within such thirty-day period. However, Drkoop.com can terminate the agreement with a 5-day notice.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a conflicting statement about the notice period for termination. The original clause requires a 30-day notice, but the addition allows Drkoop.com to terminate with only a 5-day notice, creating a direct contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.4. TERMINATION FOR CAUSE. Either party may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' written notice of a breach by the other party, provided such breach is not cured within such thirty-day period. However, Drkoop.com can terminate the agreement with a 5-day notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text stipulates a 30-day notice period for termination for cause by either party. The changed text adds 'However, Drkoop.com can terminate the agreement with a 5-day notice.' This creates an inconsistency regarding the notice period required for Drkoop.com to terminate the agreement, directly conflicting with the initial 30-day notice requirement.
